title: Password Hashing, Verification, and Upgrade · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for password hashing, verification, and upgrade and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned password credential verifier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
Select and encode Argon2id memory, time, parallelism, salt, and output parameters from current guidance and measured service budgets.
Choose scrypt or PBKDF2 only from explicit availability and compliance constraints with current calibrated parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
